notes.content: COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S) IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond Completion of Essential Skills requirements (Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language) or Cal-GETC/IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of either is strongly recommended prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley to ensure timely degree progress. More information on new polices is available on the L&S Transfer Student Applicants website. By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete : 1) The 7-course pattern (as found in the UC Transfer Admission Eligibility Courses Menu in ASSIST): · Two transferable courses in English composition; · One transferable course in mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning; · Four transferable college courses chosen from at least two of the following subject areas: o arts and humanities o social and behavioral sciences o physical and biological sciences AND 2) Major Requirements : Follow the guidance on preparation for this major below. For more information on L&S General Education requirements: For information on courses that will satisfy L&S General Education (Essential Skills and Seven-Course Breadth) requirements from your community college complete the following steps: Go to "Search below for articulation agreements: Major, department, and campus-specific general education agreements” Click on the down arrow to select: Academic Year, Institution: UC Berkeley , and Agreements with Other Institutions: (Your CA Community College). Click on “View Agreements”. Under “View Agreement by” select “ General Education/Breadth ”. Click on the “General Education/Breadth” radio button, then click on “View Agreement” Learn more from the UC Berkeley Office of Undergraduate Admission and specifically about transfer admission preparation. Review all available majors at UC Berkeley .
notes.contentHtml:
COLLEGE ADMISSIONS REQUIREMENTS FOR TRANSFER STUDENTS 


THIS MAJOR IS OFFERED BY THE COLLEGE OF LETTERS AND SCIENCE (L&S)


IMPORTANT information for students applying for admission to L&S for the fall 2025 application cycle and beyond


Completion of Essential Skills requirements (Reading & Composition, Quantitative Reasoning, and Foreign Language) or

Cal-GETC/IGETC will no longer be a requirement for admission; however, completion of either is strongly recommended

prior to enrollment at UC Berkeley to ensure timely degree progress. More information on new polices is available on

the L&S Transfer Student Applicants website. 


By the end of the spring term preceding fall enrollment at Berkeley, you must complete: 


1) The 7-course pattern (as found in the UC Transfer Admission Eligibility Courses Menu in ASSIST):

·   Two transferable courses in English composition;

·   One transferable course in mathematical concepts and quantitative reasoning;

·   Four transferable college courses chosen from at least two of the following subject areas:

o arts and humanities

o social and behavioral sciences

o physical and biological sciences


AND


2) Major Requirements: 

Follow the guidance on preparation for this major below. 


For more information on L&S General Education requirements:


For information on courses that will satisfy L&S General Education (Essential Skills and

Seven-Course Breadth) requirements from your community college complete the following steps:

  1. Go to "Search below for articulation agreements: Major, department, and campus-specific general education agreements”
  2. Click on the down arrow to select: Academic Year, Institution: UC Berkeley, and Agreements with Other Institutions: (Your CA Community College). 
  3. Click on “View Agreements”.
  4. Under “View Agreement by” select “General Education/Breadth”. 
  5. Click on the “General Education/Breadth” radio button, then click on “View Agreement”


Learn more from the UC Berkeley Office of Undergraduate Admission and specifically about transfer admission preparation. 


Review all available majors at UC Berkeley.

notes.content: PREPARATION FOR TRANSFER AT THE JUNIOR LEVEL Students interested in the French major or minor at UC Berkeley should complete as much French coursework as possible at the community-college level. Two years of college-level French, or its equivalent, is highly recommended. All transfer students should consult the Placement Guidelines on the French Department website at https://french.berkeley.edu/undergraduate/placement-guidelines to determine the best level for initial enrollment in French at UC Berkeley. This major may require at least one additional semester of French language study at UC Berkeley (French 3 and/or French 4) before enrollment can be approved for 3rd year/Upper Division French courses. French 102 is the first course in the major and requires completion of French 1-4 or its equivalent, native fluency, or satisfactory completion of the department placement exam. Once accepted at UC Berkeley, students should refer to placement information below AND consult with the UC Berkeley Undergraduate Major Adviser in French regarding course planning (frendept@berkeley.edu). Students who have… Place initially in… passing grade in 1st semester or 2nd quarter college French French 2 passing grade in FR2 at UCB or 2nd/3rd semester or 3rd/4th quarter college* French French 3 passing grade in FR3 at UCB or 4th semester or 5th quarter college* French French 4 passing grade in FR4 at UCB or 3rd year college** French French 102 *If you are unsure of your ability to take French 3 or 4, contact the Second Year Coordinator, Vesna Rodic (vrodic@berkeley.edu). **Students who have taken 3rd year college French will need to take a placement test to confirm enrollment in French 102. Contact Vesna Rodic (vrodic@berkeley.edu). Please note: French 1-4 are not open to heritage and native speakers of French; heritage and native speakers should contact the Undergraduate Major Advisor for upper division placement. In the first week of class, students will be screened for appropriate placement , and instructors may make recommendations for alternate placement. Students unsure about their placement even given the guidelines below should consider attending the first days of class in TWO levels to self-assess for comfort, fluency, and skill. There is no lower division placement exam. However, if you have circumstances in which you believe you would place out of French 4 (i.e. AP of 5 + a year of schooling abroad, two years of schooling in a Francophone country), please contact Vesna Rodic for a placement test. No auditing is permitted in lower division French courses. Attendance is MANDATORY in the first two weeks of all lower division language classes and French 102. Students who do not attend class every day in the first week will be dropped from the class and any coursework not turned in on time during that period will not be counted. If you wish to add a French language course or change into a different section, attend the course even if it is listed as full ; there is a lot of movement in the first weeks of class, particularly in language classes. For more information: Undergraduate Advising, Department of French (510) 982-6181 frendept@berkeley.edu http://french.berkeley.edu
notes.contentHtml:
PREPARATION FOR TRANSFER AT THE JUNIOR LEVEL


Students interested in the French major or minor at UC Berkeley should complete as much French coursework as possible at the community-college level. Two years of college-level French, or its equivalent, is highly recommended.


All transfer students should consult the Placement Guidelines on the French Department website at https://french.berkeley.edu/undergraduate/placement-guidelines to determine the best level for initial enrollment in French at UC Berkeley. This major may require at least one additional semester of French language study at UC Berkeley (French 3 and/or French 4) before enrollment can be approved for 3rd year/Upper Division French courses. French 102 is the first course in the major and requires completion of French 1-4 or its equivalent, native fluency, or satisfactory completion of the department placement exam.


Once accepted at UC Berkeley, students should refer to placement information below AND consult with the UC Berkeley Undergraduate Major Adviser in French regarding course planning (frendept@berkeley.edu). 


Students who have…                                                                                                                                              Place initially in…


passing grade in 1st semester or 2nd quarter college French                                                                       French 2


passing grade in FR2 at UCB or 2nd/3rd semester or 3rd/4th quarter college* French                      French 3


passing grade in FR3 at UCB or 4th semester or 5th quarter college* French                                        French 4


passing grade in FR4 at UCB or 3rd year college** French                                                                              French 102 


*If you are unsure of your ability to take French 3 or 4, contact the Second Year Coordinator, Vesna Rodic (vrodic@berkeley.edu).


**Students who have taken 3rd year college French will need to take a placement test to confirm enrollment in French 102. Contact Vesna Rodic (vrodic@berkeley.edu).


Please note:

  • French 1-4 are not open to heritage and native speakers of French; heritage and native speakers should contact the Undergraduate Major Advisor for upper division placement.
  • In the first week of class, students will be screened for appropriate placement, and instructors may make recommendations for alternate placement.
  • Students unsure about their placement even given the guidelines below should consider attending the first days of class in TWO levels to self-assess for comfort, fluency, and skill.
  • There is no lower division placement exam. However, if you have circumstances in which you believe you would place out of French 4 (i.e. AP of 5 + a year of schooling abroad, two years of schooling in a Francophone country), please contact Vesna Rodic for a placement test.
  • No auditing is permitted in lower division French courses.
  • Attendance is MANDATORY in the first two weeks of all lower division language classes and French 102. Students who do not attend class every day in the first week will be dropped from the class and any coursework not turned in on time during that period will not be counted.
  • If you wish to add a French language course or change into a different section, attend the course even if it is listed as full; there is a lot of movement in the first weeks of class, particularly in language classes.



For more information:

Undergraduate Advising, Department of French

(510) 982-6181

frendept@berkeley.edu

http://french.berkeley.edu
